name: IndexPair
summary: Represents two indices: I and J.

constructors.name: IndexPair
constructors.summary: Initializes a new instance of IndexPair with two indices.

methods.name: Contains
methods.summary: Determines whether the IndexPair contains a specific value.

params.name: item
params.doc: The number to locate in the IndexPair.

returns.doc: true if item is found in the IndexPair; otherwise, false.

methods.name: CopyTo
methods.signature: public void CopyTo(int[] array, int arrayIndex)
methods.summary: Copies the elements of the IndexPair to an Array, starting at a particular Array index.

params.name: array
params.doc: The one-dimensional Array that is the destination of the elements copied from IndexPair. The Array must have zero-based indexing.

params.name: arrayIndex
params.doc: The zero-based index in array at which copying begins.

throws.when: arrayIndex is less than 0 or arrayIndex is greater than or equals the length of array minus 2.
The length of the array is less than 2.

methods.name: GetEnumerator
methods.summary: Gets an enumerator that goes over I and J, in this order.

methods.name: IndexOf
methods.summary: Determines the index of a specific item in IndexPair.

params.name: item
params.doc: The object to locate in the IList<T>.

returns.doc: The index, 0 for I or 1 for J of item if found in the list; otherwise, -1.

properties.name: Count
properties.summary: Returns the amount of elements in this pair of indices, which is always 2.

properties.name: I
properties.summary: Gets or sets the first, I index.

properties.name: Item
properties.summary: Gets or sets a particular index of this structure. RemarksOnly 0 and 1 are valid indices.

properties.name: J
properties.summary: Gets or sets the second, J index.
